turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Reporting an Internet Outage at Your Housing Scenario: You are contacting your housing provider or Internet company to report that the Internet is not working. You need to describe the problem, ask for assistance, and check when the issue will be fixed. Practise speaking clearly and politely in a service setting. You will need to define the chatbot’s role as a housing manager or Internet support staff. You can choose situations from the examples below; start with a simple report and try a more detailed interaction by asking about troubleshooting steps, technician availability, or service timelines. Learning objectives: • Use and ask about specific vocabulary for technical problems (e.g., router, connection, or outage). • Practise sequencing (e.g., when it happened and what you tried). • Use polite and persistent questioning to request assistance. • (Advanced option) Manage extended service interactions by troubleshooting the issue, confirming steps, and negotiating next actions. Instructions: Try to: • Describe the problem clearly. • Ask for possible causes or timelines. • Provide any relevant details (e.g., room number, service provider, etc.). • Close the chat once a solution or next step is agreed. • (Advanced option) Use the extension prompts below to have a longer conversation

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright — I’ll step in as your housing provider’s Internet support staff. Sure. What seems to be happening with your internet connection right now?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Bye.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, I’ve been having some trouble. The internet just isn't working at all in my apartment. Could you let me know when this started and if you've tried restarting your router yet?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Hi, my internet has stopped working.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. So the internet just stopped working. Could you tell me when you first noticed the issue, and have you already tried rebooting your router or any other steps on your end?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “It happened since last night and then I tried to reboot already several times.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, thanks for letting me know. Since it’s been going on since last night and you’ve already tried rebooting a few times, let’s check the next step. Can you tell me your apartment number so I can look into any building-wide issues or see if we need to send a technician out?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “So is there a technician available today?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely. Let me just note that down. Once I have your apartment number, I'll check our technician schedule and see if we can get someone out to you today. What’s your apartment number? All good? Let me know.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “So my apartment number is 255, and then do you know what the issue might be?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, apartment 251 noted. Thanks for that. It could be a local outage or just something with the line to your unit. Let me find out. Now, do you have a preferred time today for the technician to drop by, or should we just send them as soon as possible?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “SS password is good”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, as soon as possible it is! We’ll aim to get someone over to you shortly. Just hang tight and we’ll keep you posted on their arrival time. Anything else you’d like to ask while we’re setting that up?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “So what should I try before you send someone?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! One last thing you can try is just double-checking that all the cables are firmly plugged in and maybe even switching the router off for a full minute before turning it back on. But if that doesn't do the trick, no worries—we'll still send someone over. Does that sound okay to you?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “So should I wait here or will someone call me?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, you can just wait right there. Our technician will give you a quick call before they arrive, so you’ll know exactly when to expect them. Anything else you’re wondering about for now? Let me know.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “If the service isn’t fixed by today, do I get a refund or credit?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely, that's a fair question. If we can't get it fixed today, you'll definitely get a credit for the downtime on your account. We'll make sure you're covered. Anything else on your mind that I can help with? Let me know.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “So can I get this in writing from my record?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, I’ll send you a confirmation email with all these details so you have it in writing. You’ll get that shortly. Is there anything else you’d like to go over before we wrap up? Let me know.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “Is it possible to change to a different service plan?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Absolutely. We can definitely look at different service plans if you're interested. Just let me know what kind of changes you're thinking about, and I can walk you through your options. What kind of plan are you considering? Let’s figure it out.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “It's all good. Just I'd like to make a complaint. What's the process?”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, understood. If you want to make a complaint, that’s no problem at all. I'll let you know the process: you can simply let me know the details here, or I can direct you to our formal complaint form online. Which way would you prefer to do it? Let me know.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: “A formal complaint, yeah.”

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, a formal complaint it is. I’ll send you a link to our online form where you can fill out all the details. Once you’ve submitted it, our team will review and get back to you. Anything else you need before I send that over? Just let me know.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Transcript Unavailable

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course, you can always reach back out if things aren't fixed in the next hour or so. Thanks for letting me know, and we’ll do our best to sort it quickly. If there’s nothing else, just let me know anytime.
